arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola claims most Premier League clubs want Manchester City RELEGATED as defiant boss staunchly defends his side following sponsorship amendment blow
arts.sum: MIKE KEEGAN AND JACK GAUGHAN: City are locked in a battle with the top flight after they were charged with 115 breaches of financial rules, with a verdict expected in the new year.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola announces Rodri plan as he fumes at England over new injury problem
arts.sum: Rodri will parade his Ballon d'Or at the Etihad on Saturday with his manager, Pep Guardiola, discussing his recovery and how much the Premier League champions are missing their midfield linchpin

arts.ttl: ‘I love fighting for this club’: Guardiola on his drive to stay at Manchester City
arts.sum: Manager insists financial charges inspired him to stay He claims most clubs want City relegated if found guilty Pep Guardiola has said he “loves” fighting Manchester City’s corner against allegations of financial breaches and his belief in the club influenced the decision to sign a new two-year contract. City’s most successful manager ended uncertainty over his future this week when extending his reign until 2027. The 53-year-old claimed the deal was agreed during a two-hour meeting with the City hierarchy, including the chief executive, Ferran Soriano, and outgoing director of football Txiki Begiristain, and he signed for two years to avoid another round of speculation over his future in 12 months’ time. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Harry Kane tops Erling Haaland, fastest to 50 Bundesliga goals
arts.sum: Bayern had possession and chances, but the visitors defended resolutely with Augsburg goalkeeper Nediljko Labrović denying Kane, then twice Jamal Musiala to keep the game scoreless at half-time. Harry Kane became the fastest player to reach 50 Bundesliga goals, eclipsing previous record-holder Erling Haaland, with a hat trick in Bayern Munich's 3-0 win over Augsburg on Friday.

arts.ttl: “Just a weird signing” – ESPN pundit says Tottenham have made one of the most pointless transfers
arts.sum: Timo Werner’s second loan spell at Tottenham has drawn significant criticism, with pundits and fans questioning the decision to bring the German forward back to the Premier League following his underwhelming performances this season. ESPN FC analyst Don Hutchinson recently criticised the decision to bring Werner back to the Premier League, citing his minimal impact […] The post “Just a weird signing” – ESPN pundit says Tottenham have made one of the most pointless transfers appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Rodri proud to be first City player to win Ballon d’Or
arts.sum: “It’s more than a dream come true to win the Ballon d’Or,” Rodri said in his City Studios interview. “At City, of course you have team-mates, you have the coach, also the people who work in the club that don’t appear in the lights but they are there and they help you every day,” he added.

arts.ttl: Rodri: Coping with injury has been difficult
arts.sum: “But I was keeping in touch with the team-mates and watching all the games - suffering sometimes. “But always I have a positive mentality to go again and recover well.

arts.ttl: Rodri: I can’t wait to share Ballon d’Or success with the fans
arts.sum: The first City player to win it and on Saturday I will be with them and I will enjoy this with them,” he declared. And Rodri says he’s relishing the chance to show gratitude to the supporters who he says have shown him love from his first day at the Club.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola is "probably the best manager to have ever existed on Earth", says Erling Haaland
arts.sum: Manchester City striker Erling Haaland explains what makes Pep Guardiola the best manager in the world and "probably the best to have ever existed on Earth".

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola makes Man City relegation promise amid 115 Premier League charges
arts.sum: Pep Guardiola insists he will remain Manchester City manager even if the club are relegated over alleged breaches of financial rules.
